---
title: "demo_fit"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{demo_fit}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(pcurveMix)
set.seed(1234)  # for reproducability
```

This vignette illustrates fitting the model to some observed $p$ values.

First, generate some (arbitrary) random $p$ values to serve as the dataset that is to be fit:

```{r}
ps_h0 <- runif(500)
ps_h1 <- runif(500)^4
ps <- c(ps_h0, ps_h1)
hist(ps)
```

Fit the mixture model to these ps:

```{r}
fit_results_2tailed <- fit_p_curve(ps)
print(fit_results_2tailed)
```
Alternatively, fit the same ps assuming 1-tailed that they come from 1-tailed tests:

```{r}
fit_results_1tailed <- fit_p_curve(ps, tails = 1)
print(fit_results_1tailed)
```
Select out the significant p's and refit the model using just those:

```{r}
alpha <- 0.05
ps_sig <- ps[ps < alpha]

fit_sig_results_2tailed <- fit_p_curve(ps_sig, alpha = alpha)
print(fit_sig_results_2tailed)

fit_sig_results_1tailed <- fit_p_curve(ps_sig, alpha = alpha, tails = 1)
print(fit_sig_results_1tailed)

```
